sections.title: A: Word Formation
sections.instructions: Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the space.

items.sentence: The RNLI (Royal National Lifeboat Institution) is an organisation (1) ...... (DEPEND) of government control, dedicated to saving lives at sea.
The RNLI has an active fleet of several hundred lifeboats around the length and (2) ...... (BROAD) of Britain’s coastline.
Since it was founded in 1824, the RNLI has had an extremely (3) ...... (SIGNIFY) impact on maritime safety, saving over 130,000 lives.
Because the RNLI is not funded by the government, it relies on the (4) ...... (GOOD) of the public to cover its costs, its income coming from membership fees and (5) ...... (CHARITY) donations.
Some people have raised (6) ...... (OBJECT) to this, saying that such an (7) ...... (VALUE) service should be government-funded.

The lifeboat crews are almost all volunteers. They are generally (8) ...... (KNOW) by the British people as being (9) ...... (EXAMPLE) in their (10) ...... (SELF), frequently putting their lives at risk to save others.

sections.title: B: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and eight words, including the word given.

items.sentence:
I haven’t made any firm decisions yet,’ said Laurence. open
Laurence said he …………………………………………………………… at that time.

Paul and Pete share so many similar interests. common
Paul …………………………………………………………………… Pete.
items.answer: has a lot in common with

items.sentence:
It would be in your interest to take an earlier train on Sunday. off
You would ……………………………………………………………… an earlier train on Sunday.

I’ve become extremely good at missing the rush hour over the last few weeks. fine
I’ve got missing the rush hour ………………………………………………. over the last few weeks.

Although Rudy really didn’t want to play cricket on Sunday, he agreed in the end. dead
Despite …………………………………………………………… cricket on Sunday, Rudy agreed in the end.

Karen should be made responsible for dealing with customer complaints. charge
It’s high time Karen ………………………………………………………… dealing with customer complaints.

My parents are furious with me for getting a tattoo and so have grounded me. arms
My parents, ……………………………………………………………… getting a tattoo, have grounded me.

‘I don’t think we’re going to survive, David,’ I said, but of course we did. tale
I told David I thought we ……………………………………………………… , but of course we did.
items.answer: were not going to live to tell the tale

items.sentence:
I’d prefer to come out on top in the end, though! laugh
I’d rather …………………………………………………………… in the end, though!

I wish coffee didn’t affect me. effect
If ……………………………………………………………………… me.
items.answer: coffee didn't have an effect on

sections.title: C: Cloze (Krakatoa)
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Volcanic eruption has been a constant threat to our natural environment for millions of years, but seldom in recent times (21) ...... a volcano erupted with the ferocity of Krakatoa.
Krakatoa, (22) ...... is a volcanic island group in Indonesia, erupted on 27th August 1883.

(23) ...... only was the explosion (24) ...... loud that it was heard as far away (more than 3,000 km) as Perth in Australia, but it is also recognised as (25) ...... the loudest sound (26) ...... recorded.
Tens of thousands of people in the region were killed, many (27) ...... in the enormous tsunamis which the eruption produced – tsunamis which eventually reached South Africa and the English Channel.
The explosion also had a major effect on the (28) ...... world’s weather system.
The volcanic dust in the atmosphere reduced the (29) ...... of sunlight reaching the earth’s surface, reducing global temperatures by more than one degree centigrade.
Only after five years had passed (30) ...... global temperatures begin to return to normal.

sections.title: D: One Word for Three Sentences
sections.instructions: For each question, write one word which can be used in all three sentences.

items.sentence:
He's actually quite a ...... name in the world of bathroom fittings.
It was very ...... of you to admit you were wrong.
There's no need to make such a ...... thing out of this; it's not that important.

Ted's got a bit of a ...... temper so don't say anything to upset him.
Why do I always seem to draw the ...... straw?
We're running ...... of sugar again.

I'll be with you in a ...... of days.
That's not a fact, it's a ...... of opinion.
No ...... how much you try, you'll never get me to change my mind.

You'd better get your ...... together and start learning your lines.
They were caught in the ...... of handing over the drugs.
Angie was just putting on an ...... – she wasn't really upset.

We're going to be in ...... trouble if anyone catches us.
There was ...... silence for a second, then everyone started laughing.

I'm ...... and tired of her constant criticisms.
Where have you been? We've been worried ...... !

I hope the judge makes an ...... of him and throws him in jail.
Could you give me a more specific ...... ?
As a teacher, you have to set an ...... to the children.

Am I ...... in thinking you've just moved to Basingstoke?
You've got no ...... to answer me back like that.
Do animals have the concept of ...... and wrong?

Simon, this is Angela, my ...... half.
Let's play draughts or, ...... yet, chess.
Don't you think you had ...... think about this a bit more carefully?

You're cutting it ...... , aren't you? The meeting's just about to start.
Make sure you read the ...... print before you sign.
There's a ...... line between good business practice and corruption.
My jeans ………………… in the wash and they’re too small for me now.
Would you like to arrange to pay by direct …………………?
I threw some biscuit ………………… on the ground and a whole load of pigeons swooped down and started eating them.
The insects looked and tasted so horrible, I ………………… with disgust as I tried to force them down.
Have you been ………………… against tetanus in the last ten years?

The union is seriously considering taking industrial ………………….

sections.title: F: Verb Forms
sections.instructions: Complete using the correct form of the verbs in brackets.

items.sentence: It’s high time ...... (we / get) the house painted.
...... (I / listen) to you from the start, I wouldn’t be in this mess now.
I do wish ...... (you / tell) Jim, but you have so it’s too late now, isn’t it?
...... (study) English and American literature at university for three years, she went on to a highly successful career in journalism.
Frank told me ...... (he / go) to the aquarium several times that year already, so didn’t really want to go again.
If ...... (I / drive) any faster, I’d be breaking the speed limit!
It’s time for ...... (you / do) your homework, young man.
Would you sooner ...... (I / invite) Bill and Trisha round, or don’t you mind if they come?
...... (you / be) to hear tomorrow that you’ve been offered the job, how would you react?
...... (make) to wait for over an hour to see a customer services officer put me in an extremely bad mood.

sections.title: G: Phrasal Verbs
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: I hear Lizzie's taken ...... asking people to call her Elizabeth from now on.
He may have just lost the leadership election, but I wouldn't write ...... his political career just yet.
We need to spread ...... in a long line and search the whole field thoroughly.
The initial proposals were watered ...... by the supervising committee to make them more acceptable to the board of directors.
I heard about your father's illness. He is going to pull ...... , isn't he?
You mustn't let the bigger kids push you ...... !
I don't think Doug's eating properly. He looks like he's wasting ...... .
It didn't take me long to cotton ...... to the fact that Gareth was rather unpunctual.
How do you intend to drum ...... support for this?
I give ...... ! Tell me the answer.

sections.title: H: Word Formation (Sentence Level)
sections.instructions: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals.

items.sentence: Why do they put so many ............................ in processed food? **ADD**
If we ................................... the photo, you'll see his face in much more detail. **LARGE**
Don't you think it's a bit early to ............................ blame? **PORTION**
Your comments may well have done ............................ damage to management-union relations. **REPAIR**
This flat's a lot more ............................ than the others we've seen. **SPACE**
The rumours are completely ............................ and I would urge everyone not to believe them. **SUBSTANCE**
Your credit card bill provides a record of all financial ............................ carried out in the previous month. **ACT**
Although we feel that the earth is stationary, in fact this is ............................ . We're moving through space, and spinning, all the time. **ILLUSION**
Too many families in this country still suffer from financial ............................ . **HARD**
Being the hardest natural substance, diamond is practically ............................ . **DESTROY**

sections.title: I: Correct the Wrong Word (Idioms)
sections.instructions: Each of the words in bold is in the wrong sentence. Rewrite them correctly.

items.sentence: I don’t mind whether we go out or stay in. It’s six of one and half a **blot** of the other, really.
That new office block really is a **face** on the landscape, isn’t it?
What’s it like living right in the middle of the commuter **strings**?
I hope Dad doesn’t fly off the **pride** when I tell him I crashed the car.
I found it hard to keep a straight **tape** when Mr Taylor was telling us off.
If I pull a few **dozen**, I don’t think you’ll have any problems getting planning permission.
We were hoping to open next Thursday, but we’ve been delayed by all the red **wavelength**.
This hotel really is the last **handle** in luxury.
This trophy here definitely takes **word** of place in my collection.
I’m glad we’re on the same **belt** over this; it would be awful if we disagreed.

sections.title: J: Collocations (Verb + Noun)
sections.instructions: Complete using the words in the box in the correct form. There is one word you will not need. (board • break • check • club • crack • hit • paper • put • single • summon • wear)

items.sentence: ...... down on illegal immigration is this government's number one priority.
The report completely ...... over the problem of the shortage of nurses; it just doesn't deal with it at all.
Shopkeepers in the town centre have ...... up windows in anticipation of rioting during the demonstration.
We're having a solar-panelled water-heating system ...... in next week.
The Prime Minister ...... back at critics accusing her of being soft on crime.
Three firefighters have been ...... out for bravery above and beyond the call of duty.
I'm thinking of ...... out that new jazz club tonight. Do you want to come along?
Dad was completely against me getting my ears pierced but I ...... him down in the end.
Mrs Thompson's leaving at the end of term. Shall we ...... together and get her a leaving present?
I don't know how I'm going to ...... up the courage to tell George he can't stay here after all.

sections.title: K: Cloze (Dialogue)
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Hi Penny! Long time no (101) ...... !
I haven’t had much free time recently. I’ve got such a lot (102) ...... at work at the moment.
Getting on (103) ...... of you, is it?
That’s about the (104) ...... of it, yes.
The truth is I’m my own (105) ...... enemy.
I’m going to have to cut back in the very (106) ...... future, otherwise I’ll go mad!
Well, as a (107) ...... of fact, I’m glad I ran into you.
Do feel (108) ...... to come along.
I’ll certainly (109) ...... my best.
You’re in for a (110) ...... if you do make it.

sections.title: L: Vocabulary Matching
sections.instructions: Match a word from the box with each phrase. You will not need one of the words. (bully • consultant • curator • dependant • descendant • extrovert • minister • monarch • predecessor • sibling • spouse)

items.sentence: a specialising doctor
someone who is cruel to people smaller or weaker than they are
someone who relies on you for financial support
a husband or wife
someone who did your job before you
someone who looks after objects in a museum
a relative of someone from a previous generation
someone who is lively in social situations
a brother or sister
a king or queen
I want you to give this the **tallest / highest** priority.
At long **last / end**, a washing powder that really does clean whiter than white.
Good hairdressers are a bit thin on the **land / ground** round here.
Why don't you **table / chair** a proposal at the next meeting?
Cleaner air would really improve our **quality / standard** of life.
It's just a **stomach / gut** reaction but I'd say he's lying.
The King in his graciousness will deign to **consent / grant** your request, Bolitho.
Teenagers who start smoking often do so as a result of peer **pressure / force**.
That's a **model / brand** new Rolls Royce!
The typical **nuclear / atomic** family consists of two parents and two children.

sections.title: N: Grammar (Fill in the gaps)
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Tony doesn’t really fancy going out tonight and ………………… do I, to be honest.
If you haven’t got …………………… money to buy ice creams as well as chocolate, just get the chocolate.
If …………………… the flight got in at a reasonable time.
………………… as we wanted to take the flat, we just couldn’t arrange a mortgage in time.
Even …………………… you do feel better tomorrow, it’s probably better if you don’t go to school.
The Prime Minister said that we should all carry on as normal …………………… the increased risk of attack.
The film is …………………… near as good as the book.
I do find …………………… hard to believe that it will be possible one day to travel through time.
You’re talking as …………………… you understood what Sean’s going through. You don’t, you know!
………………… did we realise that we were next on the list.
